mercury-perihelion-precession.name: Mercury's Perihelion Precession
mercury-perihelion-precession.description:
The observed precession of Mercury's perihelion (the point of closest approach to the Sun)
was approximately 43 arcseconds per century more than could be explained by Newtonian
mechanics, even accounting for perturbations from other planets. This anomaly challenged
Newton's theory of gravitation and was one of the key pieces of evidence that led to
the development of general relativity.

violated_assumptions.violation_description: Newtonian mechanics assumes gravitational force acts instantaneously and follows an exact inverse square law, but Mercury's orbit shows additional precession that cannot be explained by this assumption alone

ultraviolet-catastrophe.name: Ultraviolet Catastrophe
ultraviolet-catastrophe.description:
Classical statistical mechanics predicted that a blackbody should emit infinite energy
at high frequencies (ultraviolet and beyond), which contradicted experimental observations.
This anomaly showed that classical physics could not explain blackbody radiation and
led to the development of quantum mechanics.

violated_assumptions.violation_description: Classical mechanics assumes energy can be distributed continuously among all possible frequencies, but this leads to infinite energy at high frequencies (ultraviolet catastrophe), contradicting observations

michelson-morley-null-result.name: Michelson-Morley Null Result
michelson-morley-null-result.description:
The Michelson-Morley experiment attempted to detect the motion of Earth through the
hypothetical luminiferous ether by measuring the speed of light in different directions.
The experiment found no difference in the speed of light regardless of direction,
contradicting the ether theory and leading to the development of special relativity.

violated_assumptions.violation_description: Classical mechanics assumes time is absolute, but the Michelson-Morley experiment's null result showed that the speed of light is constant in all directions, contradicting the assumption of absolute time and space

pioneer-anomaly.name: Pioneer anomaly
pioneer-anomaly.description:
The Pioneer 10 and Pioneer 11 spacecraft exhibited an unexplained constant acceleration
towards the Sun of approximately 8.74 × 10⁻¹⁰ m/s². This anomalous acceleration was
observed over decades of tracking data and could not be explained by known forces,
including solar radiation pressure, general relativity effects, or spacecraft systematics.
The anomaly challenged our understanding of gravity in the outer solar system.

violated_assumptions.violation_description: General relativity assumes that all accelerations can be explained by spacetime curvature and known forces, but the Pioneer anomaly suggested an unexplained acceleration that might indicate new physics or incomplete understanding of gravity at large distances

pioneer-anomaly.explanation:
Year: 2012. The anomaly was largely resolved through detailed analysis showing that
anisotropic thermal radiation from the spacecraft's radioisotope thermoelectric generators
and electrical systems provided the missing acceleration. However, some residual
unexplained acceleration may still exist, suggesting possible new physics or incomplete
understanding of thermal effects.

hubble-tension.name: Hubble Tension
hubble-tension.description:
A significant discrepancy between measurements of the Hubble constant (H₀) from early
universe observations (cosmic microwave background, CMB) and late universe observations
(Type Ia supernovae, Cepheid variables). Early universe measurements suggest H₀ ≈ 67 km/s/Mpc,
while late universe measurements suggest H₀ ≈ 73 km/s/Mpc. This ~5-10% tension challenges
the standard ΛCDM cosmological model and suggests possible new physics or systematic errors.

violated_assumptions.violation_description: The FLRW metric assumes a homogeneous and isotropic universe, but the Hubble tension suggests possible spatial variations, evolution of dark energy, or new physics that breaks the cosmological principle

cosmic-blueshift.name: Cosmic Blueshift Anomaly
cosmic-blueshift.description:
While most distant galaxies show redshift due to cosmic expansion (Hubble's law),
some observations have reported unexpected blueshifts or anomalous redshift patterns
that cannot be explained by simple Hubble flow. These include local blueshifts in
certain galaxy clusters, anomalous redshift quantization, and discrepancies between
predicted and observed redshifts. This challenges the assumption of uniform cosmic
expansion and the FLRW cosmological model.

violated_assumptions.violation_description: The FLRW metric assumes uniform cosmic expansion, but blueshift observations and anomalous redshift patterns suggest possible local deviations, large-scale structure effects, or violations of the cosmological principle

cosmic-blueshift.explanation:
Most blueshift observations are explained by local peculiar velocities (galaxies moving
within clusters) rather than cosmic expansion. However, some anomalous redshift patterns
remain unexplained and may indicate large-scale structure effects, modified gravity,
or systematic measurement errors. The redshift quantization claims remain controversial
and unverified by mainstream cosmology.

earth-flyby-anomaly.name: Earth Flyby Anomaly
earth-flyby-anomaly.description:
Unaccounted for changes in spacecraft speed, both increases and decreases, observed during
Earth flybys of six different spacecraft from December 8, 1990 to August 2, 2005. The anomaly
appears as a small, centrally directed, distance-independent acceleration toward Earth, similar
to the Pioneer anomaly. All potential sources of systematic error have been modeled and cannot
account for the observed anomalies.

h0-tension.name: H0 Tension
h0-tension.description: Discrepancy between two different methods of measuring the Hubble constant: local distance ladder measurements (~73-74 km/s/Mpc) versus CMB measurements (~67 km/s/Mpc), suggesting different expansion rates and ages of the Universe

mature-galaxies-high-redshift.name: Mature Galaxies at High Redshift
mature-galaxies-high-redshift.description: JWST observations of mature, massive galaxies at redshifts z>11-15 that appear older than traditional galaxy formation models predict, challenging the standard cosmological timeline

redshift-rotational-bias.name: Redshift Bias from Rotational Velocity
redshift-rotational-bias.description: Systematic redshift difference between galaxies rotating in the same versus opposite direction relative to the Milky Way, with bias increasing at higher redshifts. This suggests redshift is not purely velocity-based and may be affected by rotational velocity or tired-light effects.

violated_assumptions.violation_description: The observed redshift bias shows that rotational velocity of the Milky Way relative to observed galaxies does have a measurable effect on redshift, contradicting the assumption that it is negligible

The redshift bias that increases with distance (redshift) suggests redshift is not purely velocity-based, as the rotational velocity of the Milky Way is constant and cannot explain distance-dependent effects

relational-quantum-mechanics.relational.name: relational quantum mechanics
relational-quantum-mechanics.relational.summary: interpretation of quantum mechanics that states quantum states are relative to observers, not absolute. proposes that there is no absolute state of a system, only states relative to other systems. resolves the measurement problem by eliminating the need for wave function collapse.

rqm-assumption-1.relational-quantum-mech.statement: quantum states are relative to observers, not absolute
rqm-assumption-1.relational-quantum-mech.rationale: there is no absolute state of a system; states only exist relative to other systems (observers)

rqm-assumption-2.relational-quantum-mech.statement: all systems are equivalent as observers
rqm-assumption-2.relational-quantum-mech.rationale: any physical system can serve as an observer; there is no fundamental distinction between observer and observed

rqm-postulate-1.relational-quantum-mecha.statement: the state of a system is only defined relative to another system (observer)

rqm-claim-1.relational-quantum-mechanics.statement: relational quantum mechanics resolves the measurement problem without requiring wave function collapse

rqm-postulate-1-limited-information.rela.statement: there is a maximum amount of relevant information that can be extracted from a system.

rqm-postulate-2-unlimited-information.re.statement: it is always possible to acquire new information about a system.

rqm-postulate-3-superposition.relational.statement: if c and b define two complete families of questions, then the unitary matrix u_cb in p(q^(i)_c, q^(j)_b) = |u^(ij)_cb|² can be chosen in such a way that for every c, b and d, we have u_cd = u_cb u_bd and the effect of composite questions is given by eq.(17).

rqm-hypothesis-1-all-systems-equivalent..statement: all systems are equivalent: nothing distinguishes a priori macroscopic systems from quantum systems. if the observer o can give a quantum description of the system s, then it is also legitimate for an observer p to give a quantum description of the system formed by the observer o.
rqm-hypothesis-1-all-systems-equivalent..rationale: this hypothesis rejects any fundamental distinction between system/observer, quantum/classical system, or physical system/consciousness. it allows any physical system to serve as an observer.

rqm-hypothesis-2-completeness.relational.statement: quantum mechanics provides a complete and self-consistent scheme of description of the physical world, appropriate to our present level of experimental observations.
rqm-hypothesis-2-completeness.relational.rationale: this hypothesis asserts that quantum mechanics is complete and does not require hidden variables or additional underlying theories.

rqm-claim-2-observer-dependence.relation.statement: in quantum mechanics, different observers may give different accounts of the same sequence of events. quantum mechanical descriptions are relative to a particular observer.

rqm-claim-3-information-theory.relationa.statement: quantum mechanics is a theory about the physical description of physical systems relative to other systems, and this is a complete description of the world. physics is the theory of the relative information that systems have about each other.

rqm-claim-4-no-absolute-state.relational.statement: the notion of observer-independent state of a system, or observer-independent values of physical quantities, is inadequate to describe the physical world beyond the ħ → 0 limit. there is no absolute state of a system, only states relative to other systems.

rqm-claim-5-planck-constant.relational-q.statement: postulate 1 (limited information) already adds planck's constant to classical physics. planck's constant can be viewed as the transformation coefficient between physical units (position × momentum) and information-theoretical units (bits).

rqm-claim-6-formalism-reconstruction.rel.statement: the full formalism of quantum mechanics can be (almost) reconstructed from postulates 1 and 2. the physical content of the general formalism of quantum mechanics is (almost) nothing but a sequence of consequences of two physical facts expressed in postulates 1 and 2.

rqm-claim-7-relational-values.relational.statement: in quantum mechanics, 'state' as well as 'value of a variable' or 'outcome of a measurement' are relational notions in the same sense in which velocity is relational in classical mechanics. all physical variables are relational.

rqm-claim-8-measurement-problem.relation.statement: the measurement problem (the unease with quantum mechanics) derives from the use of an incorrect notion: the notion of observer-independent state of a system, or observer-independent values of physical quantities. by abandoning such a notion, quantum mechanics makes much more sense.

wolpert-macready-1997-no-free-lunch.title: No Free Lunch Theorems for Optimization

no-free-lunch.name: No Free Lunch Theorems for Optimization
no-free-lunch.summary: For any algorithm, any elevated performance over one class of problems is exactly paid for in performance over another class. Average performance of any pair of algorithms across all possible problems is identical. Algorithm performance is determined by how aligned it is with the probability distribution over optimization problems.

nfl-assumption-1.statement: Combinatorial optimization: search space X and cost space Y are finite.
nfl-assumption-1.rationale: Enables formal treatment; matches digital computation.

nfl-assumption-2.statement: Oracle-based model: performance is measured only by the sample dym (ordered cost values from distinct function evaluations).
nfl-assumption-2.rationale: Avoids artefacts from algorithms that revisit points; any performance measure is a function of dym.

nfl-assumption-3.statement: Algorithms are mappings from previous samples to a new (unvisited) point; formulation is deterministic (results extend to stochastic).
nfl-assumption-3.rationale: Black-box algorithms (e.g. evolutionary, simulated annealing) fit this; branch-and-bound excluded.

nfl-claim-1.statement: For any pair of algorithms a1, a2, the sum over all cost functions f of P(dym|f,m,a1) equals the sum over all f of P(dym|f,m,a2); i.e. average performance over all problems is identical (Theorem 1).

nfl-claim-2.statement: Any algorithm performs worse than random just as readily as it performs better than random, when averaged over all optimization problems.

nfl-claim-3.statement: An algorithm's average performance is determined by how aligned it is with the probability distribution P(f) over optimization problems (geometric interpretation, Section 4).

john-bell.name: J. S. Bell
sources: Author of Bell 1964 paper On the Einstein Podolsky Rosen Paradox

bell-1964-epr-paradox.title: On the Einstein Podolsky Rosen Paradox

bells-theorem.name: Bell's theorem
bells-theorem.summary: Shows that no local hidden-variable theory can reproduce all statistical predictions of quantum mechanics. Formulates the EPR idea mathematically and proves that the requirement of locality (measurement on one system unaffected by operations on a distant system) is incompatible with QM. Any theory reproducing QM exactly must have non-local structure, with instantaneous influence between distant measurements.

bell-locality.statement: The result B for particle 2 does not depend on the setting a of the magnet for particle 1, nor A on b. (Locality: the result of a measurement on one system is unaffected by operations on a distant system with which it has interacted in the past.)
bell-locality.rationale: Bell formulates the EPR locality hypothesis [2] mathematically: if the two measurements are made at places remote from one another, the orientation of one magnet does not influence the result obtained with the other. This is the 'vital assumption' that leads to the expectation value P(a,b) = ∫ p(λ) A(a,λ) B(b,λ) dλ.

bell-hidden-variables.statement: A more complete specification of the state is effected by parameters λ (hidden variables). The result A of measuring σ₁·a is determined by a and λ; the result B of measuring σ₂·b is determined by b and λ.
bell-hidden-variables.rationale: EPR argued that predetermination of measurement outcomes implies a more complete specification than the wave function. Bell takes λ as a generic parameter (single or set, discrete or continuous) and shows that under locality, no such specification can reproduce QM.

bell-expectation-form.statement: For local hidden variables, the expectation value of the product of σ₁·a and σ₂·b is P(a,b) = ∫ p(λ) A(a,λ) B(b,λ) dλ, where p(λ) is normalized. For the singlet state, quantum mechanics gives P(a,b) = -a·b.

bell-no-local-reproduction.statement: The quantum mechanical expectation value P(a,b) = -a·b cannot be represented, either accurately or arbitrarily closely, in the form P(a,b) = ∫ p(λ) A(a,λ) B(b,λ) dλ with A(a,λ), B(b,λ) depending only on local settings and λ.

bell-nonlocality-required.statement: In any theory in which parameters are added to quantum mechanics to determine the results of individual measurements without changing the statistical predictions, there must be a mechanism whereby the setting of one measuring device can influence the reading of another instrument, however remote; the signal must propagate instantaneously, so such a theory could not be Lorentz invariant.

bell-inequality-violation.statement: Experiments measuring correlated spin components on pairs of particles in the singlet state will violate inequalities satisfied by any local hidden-variable theory (e.g. the correlation will follow -a·b and not be representable in the form (2)).
bell-inequality-violation.verification_method: Aspect et al. and other Bell-type experiments with spatially separated measurements and delayed choice.

classical-mechanics.name: Classical Mechanics
classical-mechanics.summary: Branch of physics that describes the motion of macroscopic objects under the influence of forces. Based on Newton's laws of motion and the assumption of absolute space and time.

assumptions.statement: Time is absolute and global - it flows uniformly and identically for all observers, regardless of their motion or position
assumptions.rationale: This fundamental assumption distinguishes classical mechanics from relativity, where time is relative. In classical mechanics, there is a universal 'now' that all observers share.

assumptions.statement: Space is absolute - it exists as a fixed, unchanging background independent of matter and energy
assumptions.rationale: Space is treated as a three-dimensional Euclidean space that provides the stage for physical events, but is not affected by them

assumptions.statement: Objects have definite positions and velocities that can be measured simultaneously with arbitrary precision
assumptions.rationale: Unlike quantum mechanics, classical mechanics assumes no fundamental uncertainty in position and momentum measurements

postulates.statement: Newton's First Law: An object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion stays in motion at constant velocity, unless acted upon by an external force
Newton's Second Law: The acceleration of an object is directly proportional to the net force acting on it and inversely proportional to its mass (F = ma)
Newton's Third Law: For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction

claims.statement: The laws of motion accurately describe the behavior of macroscopic objects at everyday speeds
Classical mechanics breaks down at very high speeds (approaching the speed of light) and for very small objects (atomic scale)

newtons-laws-of-motion.name: Newton's Laws of Motion
newtons-laws-of-motion.summary: Three fundamental laws describing the relationship between motion and forces

assumptions.statement: Time is absolute and global - all observers measure the same time
assumptions.rationale: Essential for defining velocity and acceleration consistently across all reference frames

universal-gravitation.name: Universal Gravitation
universal-gravitation.summary: Every particle attracts every other particle with a force proportional to their masses

assumptions.statement: Gravitational force acts instantaneously across any distance
assumptions.rationale: Assumes action at a distance without propagation delay, in contrast to general relativity where gravity propagates at the speed of light

claims.statement: Gravitational force follows an inverse-square law: F = G(m₁m₂)/r²

conservation-of-momentum.name: Conservation of Momentum
conservation-of-momentum.summary: Total momentum of a closed system remains constant in the absence of external forces

assumptions.statement: Time is absolute and uniform
assumptions.rationale: Required for momentum conservation to hold consistently across all reference frames

claims.statement: Momentum is conserved in all closed systems

conservation-of-energy.name: Conservation of Energy
conservation-of-energy.summary: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed from one form to another

assumptions.statement: Time is absolute and flows uniformly
assumptions.rationale: Energy conservation requires a consistent time coordinate across all observers

claims.statement: Total energy in an isolated system remains constant over time

noethers-theorem.name: Noether's Theorem
noethers-theorem.summary: Fundamental theorem connecting symmetries to conservation laws. States that every differentiable symmetry of the action of a physical system corresponds to a conservation law.

assumptions.statement: Physical systems can be described by an action principle
assumptions.rationale: The theorem applies to systems with well-defined Lagrangians and actions

claims.statement: Time translation symmetry implies conservation of energy
Spatial translation symmetry implies conservation of momentum

newtonian-mechanics.name: Newtonian Mechanics
newtonian-mechanics.summary: Mechanics based on Newton's three laws of motion and universal gravitation

assumptions.statement: Time is absolute and flows uniformly for all observers
assumptions.rationale: Fundamental to Newtonian mechanics - there is a universal time coordinate

assumptions.statement: Space is Euclidean and absolute
assumptions.rationale: Three-dimensional space follows Euclidean geometry and is independent of matter

postulates.statement: Newton's Laws of Motion govern the motion of all objects
Universal Gravitation: Every particle attracts every other particle with a force proportional to their masses and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them

claims.statement: Newton's laws accurately predict planetary motion and everyday mechanical phenomena

lagrangian-formulation.name: Lagrangian Formulation
lagrangian-formulation.summary: Mechanics reformulated using kinetic and potential energy in a single function

assumptions.statement: Time is absolute and flows uniformly
assumptions.rationale: The Lagrangian L(q, q̇, t) explicitly depends on time as an absolute parameter

hamiltonian-formulation.name: Hamiltonian Formulation
hamiltonian-formulation.summary: Mechanics expressed in terms of position and momentum coordinates

assumptions.statement: Time is absolute and global
assumptions.rationale: Hamilton's equations describe evolution with respect to absolute time

action-principle.name: Action Principle
action-principle.summary: Physical systems follow paths that minimize or maximize the action integral

assumptions.statement: Time is absolute and provides the integration variable for the action
assumptions.rationale: The action S = ∫L dt integrates over absolute time

analytical-mechanics.name: Analytical Mechanics
analytical-mechanics.summary: Reformulation of mechanics using variational principles and energy-based approaches

assumptions.statement: Time is absolute and global
assumptions.rationale: Analytical mechanics still operates within the framework of absolute time

differential-geometry.name: Differential Geometry
differential-geometry.summary: Mathematical field that studies geometric structures using techniques from calculus and linear algebra. Studies curves, surfaces, and higher-dimensional manifolds, their curvature, and intrinsic properties. Founded by Gauss's work on surfaces and developed by Riemann into the study of n-dimensional manifolds. Provides the mathematical foundation for general relativity and modern physics.

general-relativity.name: General Relativity
general-relativity.summary: Theory of gravitation that describes gravity as the curvature of spacetime caused by matter and energy. Replaces Newton's law of universal gravitation and provides a geometric description of gravity.

assumptions.statement: Pioneer spacecraft are excellent for dynamical astronomy studies because their spin-stabilization and great distances permit precise acceleration estimations to the level of 10^-10 cm/s² (averaged over 5 days)
assumptions.rationale: The paper states that Pioneer spacecraft require minimum Earth-attitude reorientation maneuvers, allowing precise measurement of small accelerations

assumptions.statement: All known forces acting on Pioneer spacecraft can be accurately modeled, including planetary perturbations, radiation pressure, interplanetary media, general relativity, and bias/drift in range and Doppler
assumptions.rationale: The paper states that analyses were modeled to include all these effects, suggesting they can be accounted for

assumptions.statement: Thermal radiation from RTGs and spacecraft systems is approximately isotropic
assumptions.rationale: The paper states that if thermal radiation were not isotropic and caused the acceleration, it would have decreased with time, but no such decrease was observed after 1980

assumptions.statement: The Earth flyby anomaly is caused by a small acceleration (in addition to natural gravitation), centrally directed and independent of distance, directed toward the core center of Earth
assumptions.rationale: Ellman proposes that the flyby anomaly shares the same characteristics as the Pioneer anomaly - a centrally directed, distance-independent acceleration

assumptions.statement: The Flyby Anomaly and Pioneer Anomaly share the same cause - a centrally directed, distance-independent acceleration
assumptions.rationale: Ellman argues that both anomalies exhibit the same characteristics and thus likely have the same underlying cause

assumptions.statement: A universal decay of the length dimension of all material reality causes the anomalous accelerations observed in flybys, Pioneer spacecraft, and galactic rotation curves
assumptions.rationale: Ellman proposes that a universal length decay would produce centrally directed, distance-independent accelerations without conflicting with planetary ephemeris

assumptions.statement: Spacetime is a four-dimensional manifold that can be curved by the presence of matter and energy
assumptions.rationale: This geometric view of spacetime allows gravity to be described as curvature rather than a force

assumptions.statement: The laws of physics are the same in all inertial reference frames (principle of relativity)
assumptions.rationale: Extends special relativity to include accelerated frames and gravitational fields

assumptions.statement: The speed of light in vacuum is constant and independent of the motion of the source or observer
assumptions.rationale: Carried over from special relativity as a fundamental constant of nature

postulates.statement: The equivalence principle: The effects of gravity are locally indistinguishable from the effects of acceleration
Spacetime curvature is determined by the distribution of matter and energy through the Einstein field equations: G_μν = 8πG/c⁴ T_μν
Free-falling objects follow geodesics (the shortest paths) in curved spacetime

claims.statement: There is an unmodelled acceleration towards the Sun of (8.09 ± 0.20) × 10^-8 cm/s² for Pioneer 10 and (8.56 ± 0.15) × 10^-8 cm/s² for Pioneer 11, confirmed by two independent analysis codes (JPL-ODP and CHASMP)
The magnitude of the anomalous acceleration does not vary with distance, within a sensitivity of 2 × 10^-8 cm/s² over a range of 40 to 60 AU

Potential causes ruled out include: gravity from Kuiper belt, gravity from galaxy, spacecraft gas leaks, errors in planetary ephemeris, errors in Earth orientation/precession/nutation, solar radiation pressure, and precessional attitude-control maneuvers

If the anomalous acceleration is gravitational in origin, it is not universal - it must affect bodies in the 1000 kg range more than planetary-sized bodies by a factor of 100 or more, which would violate the Principle of Equivalence
Ulysses spacecraft was subjected to an unmodelled acceleration towards the Sun of (12 ± 3) × 10^-8 cm/s², consistent with the Pioneer effect
Six different spacecraft involved in Earth flybys from December 8, 1990 to August 2, 2005 showed unaccounted for changes in spacecraft speed, both increases and decreases
The magnitude of the anomalous acceleration component parallel to the spacecraft flight path depends on latitude, with the effective acceleration being A(λ) = A·Cos[λ], where λ is the latitude

Depending on the specific flight path, a spacecraft may experience overall net anomalous acceleration or deceleration, or zero net modification if the path is perfectly symmetrical relative to the equator

The same anomalous centrally directed, distance-independent acceleration occurs relative to Earth (flyby anomaly), relative to the Sun (Pioneer anomaly), in galactic rotation curves, and in rotating groups of galaxies
A universal decay of the length dimension would not conflict with the planetary ephemeris and would not be detectable except in unusual circumstances like the Pioneer and Flyby anomalies
Light rays are bent by gravitational fields
Time passes more slowly in stronger gravitational fields (gravitational time dilation)
Gravitational waves exist and propagate at the speed of light
Black holes can form when matter collapses beyond its Schwarzschild radius
The universe can be described by cosmological models with a scale factor that evolves over time

predictions.statement: Future tests using Pioneer 10 reacquisition, further Ulysses data, Pluto Express mission, and Solar Probe mission could provide high-quality data to investigate the anomaly
predictions.verification_method: Further analysis was conducted, and the anomaly was largely resolved in 2012 through detailed thermal radiation analysis

predictions.statement: The origin of the anomalous signal might be new physics, though the probability is that some standard physics or unknown systematic will be found to explain it
predictions.verification_method: The anomaly was largely explained by anisotropic thermal radiation from spacecraft systems, not new physics

predictions.statement: Experiments to test the universal decay and measure its parameters are proposed to verify the theory

riemannian-geometry.name: Riemannian Geometry
riemannian-geometry.summary:
Riemann's foundational work introducing n-dimensional manifolds, metric tensors, and intrinsic curvature.
This paper established that space is a particular case of a triply extended magnitude, that geometry
cannot be derived from general notions of magnitude alone, and that the properties distinguishing
space must be deduced from experience. It introduced the concept of manifolds, measure-relations,
and curvature, laying the groundwork for differential geometry and later general relativity.

assumptions.statement: Space can be described as a multiply extended magnitude (manifold) of n dimensions
assumptions.rationale: Riemann constructs the notion of a multiply extended magnitude from general notions of magnitude, establishing that space is a particular case of a triply extended magnitude

assumptions.statement: The length of lines is independent of their position, and consequently every line is measurable by means of every other
assumptions.rationale: This is the hypothesis that first presents itself for measure-determinations, allowing the establishment of a mathematical expression for the length of a line

assumptions.statement: The line-element is expressible as the square root of a quadric differential expression (flatness in the smallest parts)
assumptions.rationale: Riemann restricts himself to manifoldnesses where the line element is expressed as the square root of a quadric differential, which includes the simplest cases and allows geometric representation

assumptions.statement: The properties which distinguish space from other conceivable triply extended magnitudes are only to be deduced from experience
assumptions.rationale: Riemann argues that geometry cannot be derived from general notions of magnitude alone, and that the measure-relations of space must be determined from empirical facts, which are hypotheses of empirical certainty

postulates.statement: A multiply extended magnitude is constructed by composing a variability of m+1 dimensions out of a variability of n dimensions and a variability of one dimension
The determination of position in an n-ply extended manifoldness is reduced to n determinations of quantity

The linear element ds is the square root of an always positive integral homogeneous function of the second order of the quantities dx, in which the coefficients are continuous functions of the quantities x
The measure-relations of a manifoldness are determined by the curvature at each point in ½n(n-1) surface-directions

claims.statement: Space is only a particular case of a triply extended magnitude, and a multiply extended magnitude is capable of different measure-relations
The propositions of geometry cannot be derived from general notions of magnitude, but must be deduced from experience
Manifoldnesses in which the square of the line-element may be expressed as the sum of the squares of complete differentials are called flat (Euclidean space is a special case)
The curvature at a point in a given surface-direction measures the deviation of the manifoldness from flatness at that point
Manifoldnesses with constant curvature allow figures to be moved without stretching, and the measure-relations depend only on the value of the curvature
Space is an unbounded three-fold manifoldness, but its infinite extent does not follow from this; if space has constant positive curvature, it must necessarily be finite
If the curvature at each point is zero in three surface-directions, the sum of the angles of a triangle is always equal to two right angles (Euclidean geometry)

predictions.statement: If bodies exist independently of position, the curvature is everywhere constant, and astronomical measurements should determine that it cannot be different from zero, or its reciprocal must be an area in comparison with which the range of our telescopes may be neglected
predictions.verification_method: Eddington expedition confirmed that space is approximately flat on large scales, though general relativity later showed local curvature near massive objects

predictions.statement: The metric relations of space in the infinitely small may not conform to the hypotheses of geometry, and we ought to suppose this if we can thereby obtain a simpler explanation of phenomena
predictions.verification_method: Einstein showed that spacetime curvature explains gravitational phenomena, validating Riemann's insight that geometry in the small may differ from Euclidean geometry

predictions.statement: Either the reality which underlies space must form a discrete manifoldness, or we must seek the ground of its metric relations outside it, in binding forces which act upon it
predictions.verification_method: Einstein's general relativity showed that metric relations come from the stress-energy tensor (binding forces), partially validating the second option

einstein-field-equations.name: Einstein Field Equations
einstein-field-equations.summary: Set of equations describing how matter and energy curve spacetime

assumptions.statement: The Einstein tensor G_μν is proportional to the stress-energy tensor T_μν
assumptions.rationale: This relates the geometry of spacetime (left side) to the matter-energy content (right side)

postulates.statement: The contracted Bianchi identities ensure energy-momentum conservation: ∇_μ G^μν = 0

claims.statement: The field equations reduce to Newton's law of gravitation in the weak-field, low-velocity limit

riemann-curvature-tensor.name: Riemann Curvature Tensor
riemann-curvature-tensor.summary: Fundamental tensor in differential geometry that completely characterizes the intrinsic curvature of a manifold

assumptions.statement: Space can be described as a differentiable manifold with a metric tensor
assumptions.rationale: This allows the application of differential geometry to describe curved spaces

claims.statement: The Riemann tensor measures the failure of parallel transport to be path-independent

ricci-curvature.name: Ricci Curvature
ricci-curvature.summary: Mathematical tensor that measures the degree to which the geometry of a space differs from that of flat Euclidean space

claims.statement: The Ricci tensor is a contraction of the Riemann curvature tensor

contracted-bianchi-identities.name: Contracted Bianchi Identities
contracted-bianchi-identities.summary: Fundamental differential identities in general relativity that ensure the Einstein tensor is divergence-free

postulates.statement: The contracted Bianchi identities yield ∇_μ G^μν = 0, ensuring energy-momentum conservation

claims.statement: The Einstein field equations automatically satisfy energy-momentum conservation without requiring it as a separate postulate

flrw-metric.name: FLRW Metric
flrw-metric.summary: Friedmann-Lemaître-Robertson-Walker metric describing a homogeneous, isotropic, expanding or contracting universe

assumptions.statement: The universe is homogeneous (uniform in space) and isotropic (looks the same in all directions)
assumptions.rationale: This cosmological principle simplifies the description of the large-scale universe

claims.statement: The universe can be described by a scale factor a(t) that evolves over time

black-holes.name: Black Holes
black-holes.summary: Regions of spacetime where gravity is so strong that nothing can escape

assumptions.statement: Matter can collapse to a point where the escape velocity exceeds the speed of light
assumptions.rationale: This follows from the Schwarzschild solution of the Einstein field equations

claims.statement: Black holes have an event horizon beyond which nothing can escape
Black holes emit Hawking radiation and can evaporate over time

einstein-weyl-geometry.name: Einstein–Weyl Geometry
einstein-weyl-geometry.summary: Generalization of Einstein's general relativity that incorporates conformal (scale) geometry. Proposed by Hermann Weyl as an attempt to unify gravity and electromagnetism through a conformal connection that allows for scale transformations while preserving the causal structure of spacetime.

assumptions.statement: Spacetime geometry can be described using a conformal connection that allows for scale transformations
assumptions.rationale: Weyl's conformal geometry extends Riemannian geometry to include scale transformations while preserving angles and causal structure

assumptions.statement: The electromagnetic field can be unified with gravity through the conformal connection
assumptions.rationale: Weyl proposed that the electromagnetic potential could be identified with the conformal connection, providing a geometric foundation for electromagnetism

postulates.statement: The metric tensor and conformal connection together determine the geometry of spacetime

claims.statement: Einstein–Weyl geometry reduces to standard general relativity when the conformal connection vanishes
The theory provides a geometric interpretation of electromagnetism through the conformal connection

no-free-lunch.name: No Free Lunch Theorems for Optimization
no-free-lunch.summary: For any algorithm, any elevated performance over one class of problems is exactly paid for in performance over another class. Average performance of any pair of algorithms across all possible problems is identical. Algorithm performance is determined by how aligned it is with the probability distribution over optimization problems.

assumptions.statement: Combinatorial optimization: search space X and cost space Y are finite.
assumptions.rationale: Enables formal treatment; matches digital computation.

assumptions.statement: Oracle-based model: performance is measured only by the sample dym (ordered cost values from distinct function evaluations).
assumptions.rationale: Avoids artefacts from algorithms that revisit points; any performance measure is a function of dym.

assumptions.statement: Algorithms are mappings from previous samples to a new (unvisited) point; formulation is deterministic (results extend to stochastic).
assumptions.rationale: Black-box algorithms (e.g. evolutionary, simulated annealing) fit this; branch-and-bound excluded.

claims.statement: For any pair of algorithms a1, a2, the sum over all cost functions f of P(dym|f,m,a1) equals the sum over all f of P(dym|f,m,a2); i.e. average performance over all problems is identical (Theorem 1).
Any algorithm performs worse than random just as readily as it performs better than random, when averaged over all optimization problems.
An algorithm's average performance is determined by how aligned it is with the probability distribution P(f) over optimization problems (geometric interpretation, Section 4).

relational-quantum-mechanics.name: Relational Quantum Mechanics
relational-quantum-mechanics.summary: Interpretation of quantum mechanics that states quantum states are relative to observers, not absolute. Proposes that there is no absolute state of a system, only states relative to other systems. Resolves the measurement problem by eliminating the need for wave function collapse.

assumptions.statement: Quantum states are relative to observers, not absolute
assumptions.rationale: There is no absolute state of a system; states only exist relative to other systems (observers)

assumptions.statement: All systems are equivalent as observers
assumptions.rationale: Any physical system can serve as an observer; there is no fundamental distinction between observer and observed

assumptions.statement: All systems are equivalent: nothing distinguishes a priori macroscopic systems from quantum systems. If the observer O can give a quantum description of the system S, then it is also legitimate for an observer P to give a quantum description of the system formed by the observer O.
assumptions.rationale: This hypothesis rejects any fundamental distinction between system/observer, quantum/classical system, or physical system/consciousness. It allows any physical system to serve as an observer.

assumptions.statement: Quantum mechanics provides a complete and self-consistent scheme of description of the physical world, appropriate to our present level of experimental observations.
assumptions.rationale: This hypothesis asserts that quantum mechanics is complete and does not require hidden variables or additional underlying theories.

postulates.statement: There is a maximum amount of relevant information that can be extracted from a system.
It is always possible to acquire new information about a system.

If c and b define two complete families of questions, then the unitary matrix U_cb in p(Q^(i)_c, Q^(j)_b) = |U^(ij)_cb|² can be chosen in such a way that for every c, b and d, we have U_cd = U_cb U_bd and the effect of composite questions is given by eq.(17).

claims.statement: Relational Quantum Mechanics resolves the measurement problem without requiring wave function collapse
In quantum mechanics, different observers may give different accounts of the same sequence of events. Quantum mechanical descriptions are relative to a particular observer.

Quantum mechanics is a theory about the physical description of physical systems relative to other systems, and this is a complete description of the world. Physics is the theory of the relative information that systems have about each other.

The notion of observer-independent state of a system, or observer-independent values of physical quantities, is inadequate to describe the physical world beyond the ħ → 0 limit. There is no absolute state of a system, only states relative to other systems.

Postulate 1 (limited information) already adds Planck's constant to classical physics. Planck's constant can be viewed as the transformation coefficient between physical units (position × momentum) and information-theoretical units (bits).

The full formalism of quantum mechanics can be (almost) reconstructed from Postulates 1 and 2. The physical content of the general formalism of quantum mechanics is (almost) nothing but a sequence of consequences of two physical facts expressed in postulates 1 and 2.

In quantum mechanics, 'state' as well as 'value of a variable' or 'outcome of a measurement' are relational notions in the same sense in which velocity is relational in classical mechanics. All physical variables are relational.

The measurement problem (the unease with quantum mechanics) derives from the use of an incorrect notion: the notion of observer-independent state of a system, or observer-independent values of physical quantities. By abandoning such a notion, quantum mechanics makes much more sense.

tired-light-theory.name: Tired Light Theory
tired-light-theory.summary:
Theory proposed by Fritz Zwicky that photons lose energy as they travel through space,
causing redshift without requiring expansion. In extreme form, argues the Big Bang is
an artifact and the Universe is in steady state. The theory has lacked direct empirical
evidence until potentially this observation.

claims.statement: Galaxies that rotate in the same direction relative to the Milky Way have higher mean redshift than galaxies rotating in the opposite direction, with the difference increasing at higher redshifts

The redshift bias is consistent across multiple datasets: SDSS, DESI Legacy Survey, HSC DR3, and third-party catalogs, using different telescopes, annotation methods, and covering both Northern and Southern Galactic poles
The redshift difference (Δz) between galaxies rotating in opposite directions increases with redshift, from ~0.0007-0.0016 at z<0.1 to ~0.0059-0.0070 at z=0.2-0.3

Redshift bias can explain the H0 tension: selecting SH0ES supernovae by rotation direction changes H0 from ~73.7 to ~69.05 km/s/Mpc (same direction) or ~74.2 km/s/Mpc (opposite direction), bringing same-direction value within error of CMB measurements
Galaxies rotating in opposite directions show ~10% flux differences in different filters, with larger absolute differences at shorter wavelengths

predictions.statement: The redshift bias will be more significant at higher redshifts (z>0.3) observed by JWST, potentially explaining mature galaxies in the early Universe

The observed 140% excess of galaxies rotating opposite to Milky Way in JWST deep fields is due to brightness differences from Doppler shift combined with tired-light effects that become more significant at greater distances

standard-cosmological-model.name: Standard Cosmological Model (Lambda-CDM)
standard-cosmological-model.summary:
The standard model of cosmology based on dark energy (Lambda) and cold dark matter (CDM).
Assumes redshift is primarily due to cosmic expansion and Hubble flow. Faces tensions
including H0 tension and unexpected mature galaxies at high redshifts observed by JWST.

assumptions.statement: The rotational velocity of the Milky Way galaxy relative to observed galaxies has a negligible effect on redshift measurements
assumptions.rationale: Standard redshift models assume rotational velocity is far smaller than linear velocity and can be ignored when measuring cosmological redshift

assumptions.statement: Redshift corresponds primarily to the linear velocity of objects relative to Earth due to cosmic expansion
assumptions.rationale: Standard cosmological model interprets redshift as Doppler effect from Hubble flow and cosmic expansion

quantum-field-theory.name: Quantum Field Theory
quantum-field-theory.summary: Theoretical framework combining quantum mechanics with special relativity to describe how fields and particles interact. Provides the foundation for understanding fundamental forces and particles.

assumptions.statement: Fields are the fundamental entities, and particles are excitations of these fields
assumptions.rationale: Quantum field theory treats particles as quantized excitations of underlying fields rather than point particles

assumptions.statement: Spacetime uses Minkowski (hyperbolic) geometry from special relativity
assumptions.rationale: QFT operates in flat Minkowski spacetime, requiring the hyperbolic geometry structure

postulates.statement: Fields obey quantum mechanical principles and special relativity

claims.statement: Quantum field theory successfully describes particle creation and annihilation

path-integral-formulation.name: Path Integral Formulation
path-integral-formulation.summary: Alternative formulation of quantum mechanics that describes the probability amplitude for a particle to travel between two points as a sum over all possible paths. Each path contributes with a phase proportional to the action along that path. Equivalent to the Schrödinger equation but provides intuitive insights into quantum behavior.

assumptions.statement: All possible paths between two points contribute to the quantum amplitude
assumptions.rationale: The path integral sums over all possible paths, weighted by the action, to compute quantum amplitudes

assumptions.statement: Time is absolute in the path integral formulation (though paths can vary in time)
assumptions.rationale: The path integral integrates over paths in spacetime, treating time as a parameter

claims.statement: The path integral formulation is equivalent to the Schrödinger equation
Path integrals provide intuitive visualization of quantum interference

feynman-diagrams.name: Feynman Diagrams
feynman-diagrams.summary: Graphical representation of particle interactions in quantum field theory. Lines represent particles, vertices represent interactions, and diagrams encode the mathematical expressions for scattering amplitudes.

assumptions.statement: Particle interactions can be represented as diagrams with lines and vertices
assumptions.rationale: Feynman diagrams provide a visual and computational tool for calculating scattering amplitudes

claims.statement: The simplification results from combining processes that were previously unnecessarily separated, such as processes involving virtual pairs with those involving only positive energy electrons
The space-time approach permits matrix elements to be written down directly for any specific problem without requiring the complicated machinery of Hamiltonian electrodynamics
Feynman diagrams simplify calculations of quantum field theory amplitudes

quantum-electrodynamics.name: Quantum Electrodynamics
quantum-electrodynamics.summary: Quantum field theory of electromagnetism describing interactions of light and matter. The most precisely tested theory in physics, with predictions verified to extraordinary accuracy.

assumptions.statement: The interaction between charges can be modified at short distances to make divergent integrals finite
assumptions.rationale: This modification allows systematic removal of infinities through renormalization

assumptions.statement: Observable phenomena are insensitive to the details of the short-distance modification (except at extreme energies)
assumptions.rationale: This allows taking the limit as the cut-off goes to zero for observable processes

assumptions.statement: Electromagnetic field is quantized and interacts with charged particles
assumptions.rationale: QED treats the electromagnetic field as a quantum field with photons as quanta

assumptions.statement: Spacetime uses Minkowski (hyperbolic) geometry
assumptions.rationale: QED operates in flat Minkowski spacetime consistent with special relativity

postulates.statement: Electrodynamics can be described as a direct interaction at a distance (albeit delayed in time) between charges, rather than through field oscillators
The interaction viewpoint is best for virtual quanta, while the field viewpoint is best for real quanta
The interaction between charged particles and photons is described by the QED Lagrangian

claims.statement: Matrix elements for complex processes in electrodynamics can be written down directly using a space-time approach, providing considerable simplification over conventional methods
The space-time view makes relativistic invariance self-evident, in contrast to the Hamiltonian method which obscures it
Each term in the expansion in powers of e²/ℏc can be written down and understood directly from a physical point of view
QED predicts the anomalous magnetic moment of the electron with extraordinary precision
Feynman diagrams provide a visual representation of particle interactions

renormalization.name: Renormalization
renormalization.summary: Mathematical procedure to remove infinities from quantum field theory calculations by redefining physical parameters. Essential for making QED and other quantum field theories produce finite, meaningful predictions.

assumptions.statement: Divergent integrals in quantum field theory can be systematically removed
assumptions.rationale: Renormalization provides a procedure to absorb infinities into redefinition of physical parameters

claims.statement: By modifying the interaction of electrons at short distances, all matrix elements become finite except those relating to vacuum polarization
The only effects sensitive to the modification are changes in mass and charge of electrons, which cannot be directly observed
Phenomena directly observable are insensitive to the details of the modification used (except at extreme energies), allowing a limit to be taken as the range of modification goes to zero
Renormalization makes QED predictions finite and accurate

quantum-computing.name: Quantum Computing
quantum-computing.summary: Computing paradigm that uses quantum mechanical phenomena like superposition and entanglement to perform computations. Feynman proposed using quantum systems to simulate quantum physics.

assumptions.statement: Quantum systems can be used to perform computations more efficiently than classical computers for certain problems
assumptions.rationale: Quantum superposition and entanglement provide computational advantages for specific algorithms

claims.statement: Quantum computers can simulate quantum systems efficiently

special-relativity.name: Special Relativity
special-relativity.summary: Theory of physics that describes the relationship between space and time in inertial reference frames. Based on the constancy of the speed of light and the principle of relativity.

assumptions.statement: Spacetime is flat with zero Riemann curvature and uses Minkowski geometry (hyperbolic/Lorentzian geometry) with metric signature (-, +, +, +)
assumptions.rationale: Special relativity assumes flat spacetime with hyperbolic geometry, where the spacetime interval ds² = -c²dt² + dx² + dy² + dz². The Riemann curvature tensor is identically zero (R^μ_νρσ = 0), meaning spacetime has no intrinsic curvature. The hyperbolic nature is a property of the entire 4D spacetime manifold (not just the time dimension), arising from the mixed signature that distinguishes time from space. This is fundamentally different from Euclidean geometry used in classical mechanics.

assumptions.statement: The laws of physics are the same in all inertial reference frames (principle of relativity)
assumptions.rationale: No preferred inertial frame exists; physics is the same for all observers moving at constant velocity relative to each other

assumptions.statement: The speed of light in vacuum is constant and independent of the motion of the source or observer
assumptions.rationale: This fundamental postulate leads to time dilation, length contraction, and the relativity of simultaneity

assumptions.statement: Time is relative - there is no absolute, global time. Simultaneity is relative to the observer's reference frame
assumptions.rationale: In contrast to classical mechanics, special relativity shows that time flows differently for observers in relative motion

postulates.statement: The principle of relativity: The laws of physics are identical in all inertial frames of reference
The constancy of the speed of light: The speed of light in vacuum is the same for all observers, regardless of the motion of the light source or observer

claims.statement: Length contraction: Objects in motion appear shorter in the direction of motion
Relativity of simultaneity: Events that are simultaneous in one frame are not simultaneous in another frame moving relative to the first
Mass-energy equivalence: E = mc²

predictions.statement: Time dilation: Moving clocks run slower than stationary clocks
predictions.verification_method: Hafele-Keating experiment with atomic clocks on airplanes

minkowski-spacetime.name: Minkowski Spacetime
minkowski-spacetime.summary: Four-dimensional mathematical model combining three-dimensional space with time into a single continuum using hyperbolic geometry

assumptions.statement: Spacetime is a four-dimensional flat manifold with zero Riemann curvature and Minkowski (hyperbolic/Lorentzian) geometry
assumptions.rationale: The Minkowski metric uses hyperbolic geometry with signature (-, +, +, +), fundamentally different from Euclidean geometry. The spacetime interval is ds² = -c²dt² + dx² + dy² + dz². The Riemann curvature tensor is identically zero (R^μ_νρσ = 0), meaning the manifold has no intrinsic curvature - it is globally flat. The hyperbolic structure is a property of the entire spacetime, not just the time dimension, arising from the mixed signature that enables light cones and causality.

assumptions.statement: Time and space are unified into a single four-dimensional continuum
assumptions.rationale: Time is treated as a dimension on equal footing with spatial dimensions, but with opposite sign in the metric

claims.statement: The Minkowski metric provides the geometric foundation for special relativity

lorentz-transformations.name: Lorentz Transformations
lorentz-transformations.summary: Mathematical transformations that relate space and time coordinates between different inertial reference frames

assumptions.statement: Spacetime uses Minkowski (hyperbolic) geometry
assumptions.rationale: Lorentz transformations preserve the Minkowski metric and are the isometries of hyperbolic spacetime

claims.statement: Lorentz transformations preserve the spacetime interval and the speed of light

relativistic-kinematics.name: Relativistic Kinematics
relativistic-kinematics.summary: Study of motion in the absence of forces, accounting for relativistic effects

assumptions.statement: Spacetime follows Minkowski (hyperbolic) geometry
assumptions.rationale: Relativistic kinematics is based on the hyperbolic geometry of Minkowski spacetime

relativistic-dynamics.name: Relativistic Dynamics
relativistic-dynamics.summary: Study of motion under the influence of forces, incorporating relativistic corrections

assumptions.statement: Spacetime uses Minkowski (hyperbolic) geometry
assumptions.rationale: Relativistic dynamics requires the hyperbolic metric structure of Minkowski spacetime

relativistic-mechanics.name: Relativistic Mechanics
relativistic-mechanics.summary: Mechanics reformulated to account for relativistic effects at high velocities

twin-paradox.name: Twin Paradox
twin-paradox.summary: Thought experiment demonstrating time dilation where a traveling twin ages slower than a stationary twin

assumptions.statement: Time is relative and spacetime uses Minkowski (hyperbolic) geometry
assumptions.rationale: The twin paradox demonstrates the non-Euclidean nature of time in Minkowski spacetime

claims.statement: A traveling twin will age less than a stationary twin due to time dilation
